articles.headline: NVIDIA and IREN Partner to Deploy 5 GW of AI Infrastructure
articles.summary: NVIDIA and IREN announced a strategic partnership to accelerate deployment of up to 5 GW of AI infrastructure globally. This signals major near-term capacity buildouts tied to chip-maker demand, with IREN's European expansion providing clearer commercialization timelines for businesses planning AI infrastructure investments.

articles.headline: Google's TurboQuant Algorithm Could Reshape AI Memory Costs and Adoption
articles.summary: Google's TurboQuant dramatically reduces memory requirements for running AI systems, triggering immediate stock volatility in memory chipmakers. For businesses planning AI adoption, this innovation signals potential cost reductions but also uncertainty in hardware pricing and infrastructure ROI planning throughout 2026.

articles.headline: Industries Most Affected by AI in 2026: How Artificial Intelligence Is Changing Work
articles.summary: AI is transforming IT, retail, manufacturing, and logistics through automation of coding, inventory forecasting, predictive maintenance, and route optimization, boosting productivity while reshaping jobs. Businesses from SMEs to corporations can leverage these tools to cut repetitive tasks, improve efficiency, and manage workforce transitions. Adoption varies by sector, with strong impacts on operational costs and competitiveness.
